* Fix celestial theme polish regressions

* review: adopt justified PR feedback

- remove redundant fade-in on always-mounted chat UI to avoid misleading mount animations
- update celestial theme spec metadata and notes to reflect implemented state and non-prescriptive guidance

Evaluated against PR review recommendations.

* Fix MentionInput parser regression

Repair the malformed merge artifact in MentionInput that was breaking frontend lint in PR 2651.

The failing GitHub Actions run reported a parser error in frontend/src/components/chat/MentionInput.tsx. The file had duplicated fragments from two versions of the component: an imperative handle block had been spliced into the mount effect, the keydown callback was missing its closing brace before the dependency array, and the placeholder JSX tail contained both the newer responsive placeholder path and an older duplicate fallback block.

This change removes the duplicated fragments and restores the intended component structure without altering runtime behavior. The final file keeps the existing responsive placeholder support, cycling placeholder rendering, and imperative handle API, but returns to a syntactically valid single implementation.

Validation: ran frontend lint locally with npm run lint in the PR 2651 worktree, and eslint completed successfully after the repair.

refactor: split manael.go and manael_test.go into domain-specific files (#1695)

- [x] Create `format.go` with `isAPNG`, `skipGIFSubBlocks`,
`isAnimatedGIF`, `pngSignature`
- [x] Create `header.go` with `setVaryHeader`, `avifEnabled`,
`scanAcceptHeader`, `check`, `updateContentDispositionFilename`
- [x] Create `options.go` with `ProxyOptions`, `ProxyOption`, and all
`With...` functions
- [x] Create `proxy.go` with `ctxKey`, `resizeProxy`, `convert`,
`modifyResponse`, `NewServeProxy`
- [x] Delete `manael.go` (content moved to above files)
- [x] Create `proxy_test.go` (package manael_test) with core proxy
integration tests
- [x] Create `options_test.go` (package manael_test) with
options/resize/quality integration tests
- [x] Restore `fuzz_test.go` (package manael) with all `Fuzz*` tests —
fuzz tests are a distinct concept from unit tests
- [x] Remove `format_test.go` and `header_test.go` (contained only fuzz
tests, now consolidated in `fuzz_test.go`)
- [x] Delete `manael_test.go`
- [x] Update copyright years to 2026 in all newly created files
- [x] Restore accidentally modified `website/go.mod` and
`website/go.sum`
- [x] Verify `go test ./...` passes
- [x] Fix logic bug in `check()`: changed `&&` to `||` so non-GET
requests OR non-200/304 responses correctly return `"*/*"` (CodeRabbit
review)

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Refactor: Split manael.go and manael_test.go into
domain-specific files</issue_title>
> <issue_description>### Background
> Currently, `manael.go` (approx. 470 lines) and `manael_test.go`
(approx. 1000 lines) have become monolithic. They mix multiple
responsibilities including core proxy routing, HTTP header manipulation,
functional options configuration, and image format detection.
> This reduces maintainability, makes it difficult to locate specific
tests, and deviates from the Go idiom of keeping small, focused files
with a strict 1:1 mapping between implementation (`.go`) and test files
(`_test.go`).
> 
> ### Proposal
> Refactor the monolithic `manael.go` and `manael_test.go` by splitting
them into distinct, domain-specific files. This is a pure refactoring
task; no business logic or external APIs will be changed. This
reorganization will significantly improve code readability and make
future contributions easier.
> 
> ### Tasks
> Split the codebase into the following file pairs:
> 
> - [ ] **`proxy.go` / `proxy_test.go` (Core Proxy Logic)**
> - Move `NewServeProxy`, `resizeProxy`, `modifyResponse`, and
`convert`.
> - Move the basic routing, error handling, and proxy integration tests.
> - [ ] **`options.go` / `options_test.go` (Configuration)**
> - Move `ProxyOptions` struct, `ProxyOption` type, and all `With...`
functional options.
>   - Move configuration-related unit tests.
> - [ ] **`format.go` / `format_test.go` (Format Detection)**
>   - Move `isAPNG`, `isAnimatedGIF`, and `skipGIFSubBlocks`.
>   - Extract and move related unit/fuzz tests for format detection.
> - [ ] **`header.go` / `header_test.go` (HTTP Header Manipulation)**
> - Move `scanAcceptHeader`, `check`, `setVaryHeader`, `avifEnabled`,
and `updateContentDispositionFilename`.
> - Move header-specific tests (e.g., `Vary`, `Content-Disposition`, and
`Accept` header parsing tests).
> 
> ### Note for AI Agents
> Please ensure that the `package manael` declaration is kept at the top
of all new files, and verify that `go test ./...` passes successfully
after the files are moved.</issue_description>
>

commit.message:
Merging changes from OKOK to main (#22)

* Set CAN ids and pneumatic channels, tested intake feed, hopper feed, and turret uptake. Intake feed and uptake have correct inversion and max speeds now, but a gearbox is needed for the hopper feed. Flywheel motors will need to be changed to be spark flex instead of TalonFX

* Updated TurretReal to use a SparkFlex for the flywheel motors and SparkMax for the hood. Updated methods in ITurret to use safer WPILib Units. Refactored Turret to use SparkMax and SparkFlex's internal pid rather than a software pid.

* Add TODO comments to ClimbReal and TurretReal for motor conversion

* Converted main climb motor and turret rotator to their correct motor types. Coppied code from the turret flywheel conversion and combined it with the previous flywheel conversion.

* Cleaned up left over code and reverted OperatorInterface back to its normal state

* Reworked Climb subsystem to utilize BooleanEvents to stop climb only when limit switch value is rising. Added automatic detection of when the climb has reversed direction (the motor has gone too far) once the climber hits the limit switch again when going a direction where it shouldn't be able to hit the limit switch (commanded to go up, but hits the limit switch). This reverses the direction of inputs to maintain functionality and is utilized to stop the climber from over rotating and winding down into itself. Replaced the little PID implementation that we had with a basic distance check. This will be a lot easier to tune than PID and should work well enough for our use case. Updated Container to wait until the Climb hits the limit switch instead of just waiting an arbitrary amount.

* Fixed getting the climber extension, now correctly divides by the gear ratio

* Converted the pneumatics system to a REV Pneumatic Hub, setup hybrid compressor control with placeholder values from WPILib's docs, and added basic logging for the pneumatic system's pressure

* Begun work on pathplanner and container commands, added turretAtSpeed function

* Fixed climbing wrongly logic

* Refactor vision estimation processing and update turret flywheel velocity assignment

* Add setYawSensorPosition method to ITurret interface and implementations

* swerve devices on canivore

* mapped canivore bus name

* Increase update frequency for drive and steering status signals to improve responsiveness

* Remove unused suppression annotation in Hopper class and clean up MutVector class by removing TODO for unit tests

* Refactor turret subsystem to improve targeting logic and LED pattern updates; moved PwmLEDs class and slightly reorganized LED handling.

* Remove unused SysId routine for turret yaw characterization and related command method

* Enhance turret control logic to ensure dead field zone operation only when FMS is attached and maintain flywheel control regardless of dead zone status

* Update toggleShooterOn command to ensure flywheel, yaw, and hood are all on target before feeding

* Implement full limelight pose targeting support in turret subsystem; added pose correction and updated LL camera inputs

* Commented stuff that disables Swerve, added commands for Turret, added Turret_Gear_Ratio math instead of ticks per Turret Degree.

* Fixed merge conflicts on TurretMap, uncommented Climb Controls including Friction Brake, will need to check LIMIT_SWITCH_CHANNEL to get it out of its placeholder.

* Enabled Climb subsystem, set up Climb test Commands, and added the new hopper feed motor to HopperReal.java

* Climb maxmotion (#17)

* Turret State Machine Redesign + LED Overhaul (#18)

* Tested hopprer feed with the new motor, climb with simple setting percent out, and intake

* Refactor Swerve initialization, update ClimbMap solenoid channels, and modify Turret control logic for testing

* Add faceAwayFromHubCommand to Swerve subsystem (#19)

* Started implementing logic to convert from hood angle to pinion rotations for pid calculations

* Started tuning swerve and set correct module offsets

* Reworked the applyConfig method in SwerveModuleReal to start the retry loop in a fixed thread pool, sleep the thread between each attempt, then close the pool after configuration ends

* Combined the individual thread pools into a single configuration pool in SwerveIOPackager

* Adjusted turret logic to add hood homing and corrected hood MAXMotion setting

* Autonomous Paths And Universal Pathplanner Folder (#20)

* Add commands to toggle intake and climb arm states

* fixed capitalization

* Added more commands and implemented Pathplanner into the project. PLEASE MAKE SURE TO SET YOUR PROJECT FOLDER IN PATHPLANNER TO THIS ONE!

* Begun working on mutliple different autos

* Add new autonomous routines for left-to-right and right-to-left paths

* Add new autonomous routines and update path configurations for specifically climbing and shooting

* Moved everything to correct location

* removed duplicate deploy

* Enhance OperatorInterface with sysIdDriveCommand for dynamic and quasistatic testing; update Pneumatics to enable compressor; refine Swerve module logging with position data; adjust SwerveMap for steering PID constants; modify SwerveModuleReal to use RemoteCANcoder; update TurretMap with hood gear ratio; clean up TurretReal configuration; add datalogtool.json for logging setup.

commit.message:
fix: update stale client reference after reconnect in SendPromptAsync (#325)

## Bug
Multi-agent orchestrator sessions (e.g. "PR Review Squad-orchestrator")
fail to recover after connection drops, showing:
```
⚠️ Session disconnected and reconnect failed: Client not connected. Call StartAsync() first.
```

## Root Cause
In `SendPromptAsync`'s reconnect path, a local `client` variable is
captured from `GetClientForGroup()` **before** the client is recreated.
After the code disposes the old `_client` and creates a new one (lines
2408-2413), the local `client` still points to the **old disposed**
`CopilotClient`. The subsequent `client.ResumeSessionAsync()` /
`client.CreateSessionAsync()` calls use the stale reference, throwing
`InvalidOperationException: Client not connected. Call StartAsync()
first.`

```
Line 2385:  var client = GetClientForGroup(...)  // captures OLD _client
Line 2408:  await _client.DisposeAsync()          // disposes old
Line 2411:  _client = CreateClient(...)           // creates NEW _client  
Line 2412:  await _client.StartAsync(...)         // starts new
            // BUG: `client` still points to OLD disposed _client!
Line 2444:  await client.ResumeSessionAsync(...)  // uses STALE ref → crash
```

## Fix
One-line addition: `client = _client;` after successful client
recreation, so the resume/create calls use the new connected client.

## Tests
Added 5 regression tests in `ConnectionRecoveryTests.cs`:
- `GetClientForGroup_ReturnsCurrentClient_AfterReconnectClientIsNew`
- `IsConnectionError_DetectsOrchestratorDispatchError`
- `IsConnectionError_DetectsConnectionLostFollowedByNotConnected`
- `SendPromptAsync_DemoMode_ConnectionRecovery_SucceedsWithNewClient`

All 2,283 tests pass. Mac Catalyst build succeeds.

commit.message:
feat: multi-timeframe filter + mandatory volume gate (hit rate fix)

Multi-timeframe (MTF) hard gate:
- Resample 2-min bars to ~16-min (~15-min) and 60-min candles internally
- 15-min trend: EMA(5) vs EMA(8) on ~16-min candles (needs 9 candles = 72 bars)
- 1-hour trend: last close vs previous close (needs 2 candles = 60 bars)
- Hard gate: SELL suppressed if both 15-min AND 1-hour are bullish
             BUY  suppressed if both 15-min AND 1-hour are bearish
- Avoids trading against the bigger trend — #1 cause of bad signals
- min_bars bumped from 36 to max(36, 9 * mtf_15m_bars) = 72

Mandatory volume gate:
- Signal suppressed if current bar volume < volume_min_mult (default 0.8×)
- Near-zero volume bars = no market conviction = unreliable signals
- vol_ratio=None (insufficient lookback) passes through (don't gate on missing data)
- Volume spike (+1) for >= 2.0× remains as scoring bonus

Force output improvements:
- New Multi-timeframe section shows 15-min and 1-hour trend with icons
- Suppressed signals now show WHY (gate reason) vs score-too-low
- Volume gate warning shown in force output when active

instruments.json: added mtf_15m_bars (8), mtf_1h_bars (30), volume_min_mult (0.8)

commit.message:
fix: reorder noise filter after LLM review and preserve section boundaries

Two fixes to the sentence extraction pipeline:

1. Move _is_noise_sentence() from extraction loops (pre-LLM) to
   lazygraphrag_pipeline.py (post-LLM). The correct sequence is now:
   split → preamble join → LLM review → noise filter → embed.
   Only paragraph-source sentences are filtered; table rows unaffected.

2. Replace heading-stripping regex from empty string to paragraph break
   (\n\n) in _clean_chunk_text_for_spacy(). This preserves section
   boundaries so the sentence splitter doesn't merge text across
   sections. Also adds cleanup for colon-period artifacts (:.) that
   arise when colon-terminated preambles precede stripped headings.

commit.message:
feat: Phase 9 - end-to-end compilation pipeline

Programs actually compile and run for the first time:
- Build pipeline: .axon → parse → typecheck → MIR → LLVM IR → clang → executable
- C runtime library with print, alloc, panic, tensor stubs
- Main wrapper (_axon_main) with C ABI entry point
- C compiler detection (clang/gcc/cc with Windows LLVM fallback)
- Two-pass type checking for recursive/mutual function support
- TAST type resolution from checker's symbol table
- MIR type inference for chained expressions
- 15 E2E tests: empty main, arithmetic, if/else, while, fibonacci,
  multi-function, nested-if, let-binding, composed calls, mutual recursion

commit.message:
docs: update SECURITY.md with supported versions and reporting guidelines (#1697)

With the v3.0 release, the existing one-liner security policy is
insufficient — v2.x.x will continue receiving security and critical bug
fix support, and reporters need clearer guidance.

### Changes
- **Supported Versions table**: explicitly marks `3.x.x` (active),
`2.x.x` (security/critical fixes only), and `< 2.0.0` (unsupported)
- **Reporting guidelines**: instructs reporters to use
`contact@manael.org` instead of public issues, and lists expected
information (version, repro steps, impact)

commit.message:
Fix cpuinfo cross-validation: warn instead of fail when kernel hides a feature

The kernel may not expose all CPUID features in /proc/cpuinfo (e.g. CET/shstk
requires kernel support). Changed the 'we detect but kernel does not' direction
to a warning. The reverse direction (kernel has it, we miss it) remains a hard
failure since that indicates a real detection bug.
